purpose: llama.cpp (Vulkan + SYCL) vs OpenVINO GenAI on the SAME box — the scenario openvino#36270 has not measured: dense-hybrid Qwen3.6-27B on Lunar Lake / Arc 140V / Windows 11; plus the 35B-A3B MoE scope-add (the thread's model class).

hardware.gpu: Intel Arc 140V iGPU (16GB visible, uma:1, fp16:1, warp 32, KHR_coopmat detected, subgroup 16 -> INTEL_XE2 path)

software.llamacpp: b9957 (commit c4ae9a88f), official win-vulkan-x64 + win-sycl-x64 release binaries (SYCL zip bundles the oneAPI runtime)
software.openvino_baseline: OpenVINO GenAI 2026.2.1, official Qwen3.6-27B INT4 IR, measured 2026-07-08 same box/driver (docs/performance/benchmark_vlm_text_qwen3.6-27b-int4-ov_2026-07-08_16-21-18.json)

models.dense: unsloth/Qwen3.6-27B-GGUF IQ4_NL (4.5 bpw, 16.06 GB) — closest analog to the OV INT4 IR (quantizations differ; named caveat)

methodology: llama-bench -ngl 99 -p 512 -n 128 -r 3 -o json; box lean (AO + OVMS down); models loaded fresh per run; validity checks: (1) layer placement verified via verbose llama-completion load log 'offloaded 65/65 layers to GPU' on the dense 27B Vulkan path — NO CPU fallback on the Gated-DeltaNet ops (the qwen3_5moe #19957 class); (2) coherence spot-check greedy completion produced clean English; (3) live generation rate during the coherence probe (1.7 t/s) consistent with the bench median.

openvino_int4_ir.note: 2026-07-08 baseline, median sustained; not re-measured tonight (same box/driver, 2 days old — named caveat)

openvino_unofficial_int4_ir.ir_source: Morteza89/qwen3.6-35b-a3b-int4-ov (UNOFFICIAL community conversion — the official optimum-cli export re-test is the confirmation step)
openvino_unofficial_int4_ir.coherence: CLEAN — correct English answers across captured outputs; minor stray think-tag template artifacts; NOT the genai#3870 incoherence class

headline: SYMMETRIC ARCHITECTURE-DEPENDENT INVERSION: on the dense-hybrid 27B OpenVINO leads llama.cpp by 2.4-4.3x (Vulkan) and worse (SYCL); on the 35B-A3B MoE llama.cpp leads OpenVINO ~4.6-5.1x on decode and ~40-60x on prefill (OV unofficial IR: 4.0 pp / 1.59 tg vs Vulkan 245.8/7.37 and SYCL 156.5/8.14 — SYCL flips from worst-on-dense to best-decode-on-MoE). Every backend wins somewhere; no stack is uniformly faster on this silicon.
not_measured: OpenVINO on the MoE via the OFFICIAL optimum-cli export (tonight's OV MoE number is the unofficial community IR — conversion pessimality cannot be excluded until the official artifact is re-tested)
the thinking-toggle probe under llama.cpp (this build's CLI tools are chat-first/interactive; a proper llama-server probe is the follow-up)
flash-attn ON variants (ran default per Intel-iGPU stability guidance)
quantization parity (IQ4_NL/IQ4_XS GGUF vs OV INT4 IR are closest analogs, not identical)
sustained-thermal envelope (single evening; r=3 per cell)
tonight's numbers with the VM actually off (the guest was resident ~15:15 onward - see environment_correction)
environment_correction_2026-07-10_2300: HONESTY ADDENDUM: the sealed BlarAI-Orchestrator Hyper-V guest was RUNNING (started ~15:15, discovered ~22:45 by the overnight session) during ALL llama.cpp and OV-MoE measurements tonight - the box was NOT fully lean as originally stated. Internal comparisons unaffected (all tonight rows shared the environment); the dense-27B OV baseline (2026-07-08) was measured VM-Off, so those cross-day rows carry this environmental delta. Magnitude judged small vs the 2-60x effects measured (all loads fit; stddevs small); tomorrow production-model benches run VM-Off and can spot-check any close comparison.
